---
number-sections: false
---

```{=latex}
% Quarto auto-emits \chapter*{Dedication} at the top of this file. We then use
% a TikZ overlay anchored to (current page.center) to place the italic text at
% the exact vertical mid-line of the page, independent of the chapter heading's
% spacing. This is the MIT Press dedication convention: heading at top of page,
% epigraph block optically centered.
\thispagestyle{empty}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=center, align=center, text width=0.72\paperwidth]
  at (current page.center)
  {\itshape
   To the loving memory of my father,\\
   whose passion for education and unwavering commitment to quality\\
   challenge me daily to strive for excellence.\\[1em]
   And to my mother,\\
   who taught me, by instinct more than instruction,\\
   that knowledge---like everything else worth having---\\
   only matters if you give it away.};
\end{tikzpicture}
\null\clearpage
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

# Dedication {.unnumbered}

*To the loving memory of my father, whose passion for education and unwavering commitment to quality challenge me daily to strive for excellence. And to my mother, who taught me, by instinct more than instruction, that knowledge---like everything else worth having---only matters if you give it away.*

:::
